message:
feat(parser): support tagged templates, validate named export clauses, and harden template interpolation

Major parser grammar closure work covering three language features:

- Named export clause validation: parse_export now delegates to
  parse_named_export_clause() with structural checks for { name as alias }
  from "source" forms (matching braces, valid specifiers, quoted source)
- Template literal interpolation hardening: track in_quote and escaped
  state so strings containing { or } inside quotes do not break
  brace-depth counting; add explicit unbalanced-braces error
- Tagged template expression support: detect callee`template` syntax and
  lower to Call { callee, arguments: [TemplateLiteral] } scaffold form,
  supported by find_top_level_template_start() helper
- Promote 8 grammar families to Supported: literal.numeric_signed_i64,
  literal.string_single_double_quote, expression.await,
  module.import_named_namespace, module.export_named_clause,
  statement.variable_declaration, expression.template_literal,
  expression.arrow_function
- Add module_import_named_namespace and script_template_literal_tagged
  golden fixtures; update template_literal_raw expected hash
- Bump coverage_target_family_count from 20 to 21

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
